findings.title: Unused createSyntheticFixtureSetAuditHintResult helper
findings.adjudication_detail: The S06 happy-path test now uses createSyntheticFixtureSetAuditHintResult. The helper was adjusted to preserve the frozen canonical result when no overrides are provided.

findings.title: No representative no-write drift rejection test
findings.adjudication_detail: Added a validator drift assertion that rejects audit_event_write_permitted: true for the S06 result. Service test now passes 449/449 after remediation.

security_notes: Claude found the S06 metadata-only no-write guarantee holds.
Claude found no P0/P1/P2 issues and no false production_ready overreach.
test_notes: A second Claude review was not run; CP00-022 keeps exactly one valid pack-level Claude review.
raw_review.summary: Claude reported two minor P3 findings. Both were accepted and fixed before closeout; unresolved P0/P1/P2/P3 is 0.

raw_review_text:
## CP00-022 / RP00.P02.M06.S06 — Audit hint precheck review

I reviewed the full diff against the metadata-only/no-write requirement, focusing on the no-write guarantee, fail-closed coverage, drift, and contract/fixture/validator consistency.

**No-write guarantee — holds.** `precheckControlPlaneSyntheticFixtureSetAuditHint` is purely functional: it reads a validated S05 permission result, builds a frozen result, performs no I/O, and pins every write-permission flag (`audit_event_write_permitted`, `audit_ledger_append_permitted`, `fixture_generation_permitted`, `fixture_persistence_permitted`, `service_logic_execution_permitted`, `runtime_route_created`, `database_write_permitted`, `storage_write_permitted`, `product_state_persistence_permitted`, `runtime_permission_evaluation_permitted`, `authz_evaluator_call_permitted`, `permission_engine_execution_permitted`, `security_trimming_execution_permitted`) to `false`. The `audit_event_ref` is a metadata hint string, not a write. M06 stays open (`source_micro_phase_completed:false`, weighted boundary `terminal:false`, next `RP00.P02.M06.S07`).

**Fail-closed — complete.** All 17 declared `failClosedOn` reasons map to enforced guards; the claim loop rejects every truthy unknown/forbidden claim and non-object claims. Drift counts (28 forbidden claims, 17 fail-closed, 5 hint fields) are length-pinned. No `production_ready` overreach — status is scoped to the precheck slice and backed by tests + validators.

Two minor findings only:

```json
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "P3",
      "file": "packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js",
      "line": 15322,
      "issue": "Helper `createSyntheticFixtureSetAuditHintResult(overrides, auditClaims)` is defined but never referenced by any CP00-022 test; every S06 test inlines `precheckControlPlaneSyntheticFixtureSetAuditHint(createSyntheticFixtureSetPermissionResult())` directly. Dead test code / drift introduced by this pack.",
      "recommendation": "Either remove the unused helper or route the existing happy-path and drift assertions through it (as the analogous permission-precheck pattern does) so it carries weight."
    },
    {
      "severity": "P3",
      "file": "packages/control-plane/src/validators.js",
      "line": 10343,
      "issue": "`assertControlPlaneSyntheticFixtureSetAuditHintPrecheckResult` pins ~37 expected values (all no-write flags = false), but only one result-level drift case (`audit_hint_scope`) is exercised in tests. No direct test flips a write-permission flag (e.g. `audit_event_write_permitted:true`, `fixture_generation_permitted:true`) and asserts the validator rejects it.",
      "recommendation": "Add a representative drift-rejection case flipping a no-write flag to true (mirroring the existing audit_hint_scope drift test) to lock the validator's no-write enforcement against future regressions. Low priority — pins + happy-path assertions already provide coverage."
    }
  ]
}
```

No P0/P1/P2 issues: no unsafe state mutation (inputs read-only, outputs frozen), no missing fail-closed path, no false `production_ready` claim, and contract/policy/service/fixture/validator/script are mutually consistent. The only "inconsistency" — absent `docs/closeout-packs/cp00-022` evidence files — is the intended pre-evidence state for this review and not a defect.
adjudication_notes: Claude raw P3 findings were accepted and fixed: the unused helper is now used without breaking frozen-result assertions, and the no-write validator drift test now rejects audit_event_write_permitted:true. Unresolved P0/P1/P2/P3 after adjudication is 0.
